name: Attic ladder
description: A minimal vertical connector that provides access from a closet, hallway, or back-of-house corner up to an attic or loft above. Read as a utilitarian threshold rather than a circulation route: either a single fixed ladder pinned to the wall or a short, very steep stair (one block of run per block of rise) tucked into a 1x1 or 2x1 footprint. A trapdoor sits in the ceiling above the ladder head so the loft can be sealed off; in cottages and farmhouses this trapdoor is often hinged or retractable so the ladder can be pulled up and the opening closed. The footprint is deliberately small so the connector consumes almost no usable floor area in the room it springs from.
